00030 #ifndef _SYSCALL_H_
00031 #define _SYSCALL_H_
00032 
00033 
00034 struct trapframe; /* from <machine/trapframe.h> */
00035 
00036 /*
00037  * The system call dispatcher.
00038  */
00039 
00040 void syscall(struct trapframe *tf);
00041 
00042 /*
00043  * Support functions.
00044  */
00045 
00046 /* Helper for fork(). You write this. */
00047 void enter_forked_process(struct trapframe *tf);
00048 
00049 /* Enter user mode. Does not return. */
00050 void enter_new_process(int argc, userptr_t argv, vaddr_t stackptr,
00051                        vaddr_t entrypoint);
00052 
00053 
00054 /*
00055  * Prototypes for IN-KERNEL entry points for system call implementations.
00056  */
00057 
00058 int sys_reboot(int code);
00059 int sys___time(userptr_t user_seconds, userptr_t user_nanoseconds);
00060 
00061 /* File system calls */
00062 int sys_open(const char* filename, int flags, int* retval);
00063 int sys_write(int fd, const void* buf, size_t nbytes, int* retval);
00064 int sys_read(int fd, const void* buf, size_t buflen, int* retval);
00065 int sys_close(int fd);
00066 int sys_dup2(int old_fd, int new_fd, int *retval);
00067 off_t sys_lseek(int fd, off_t pos,int whence,int *retval);
00068 
00069 /* Thread system calls */
00070 void _exit(int exitcode);
00071 pid_t waitpid(pid_t pid, int *status, int options, int* retval);
00072 pid_t sys_fork(struct trapframe *tf, int *retval);
00073 pid_t sys_getpid(int* retval);
00074 int sys_execv(const char *program, char **args);
00075 
00076 #endif /* _SYSCALL_H_ */
